summary: Frontend-only verification of portrait regeneration bug-fix. About-section portrait now renders from the single hardcoded source /assets/anil_professional.png, the front/side pose toggle has been completely removed, and the old image files anil_front.png and anil_side.png are no longer referenced anywhere in the app. All sanity checks (nav links, hero, contact CTAs) pass. Zero console/page errors.

failed_requests: cdn-cgi/rum (Cloudflare RUM analytics – unrelated, expected to be blocked)

observations: GET /assets/anil_front.png and /assets/anil_side.png return HTTP 200 but with content-type text/html (the SPA catch-all serves index.html for unknown paths). The image files are physically deleted from /app/frontend/public/assets/ (only anil_professional.png remains) and nothing in the rendered HTML references those paths anymore. So the requirement 'old files should not exist / not be served as images' is functionally met; the 200/HTML response is a side-effect of the SPA fallback, not a regression.
critical_code_review_comments: About.jsx is clean post-fix: useState removed, single hardcoded portrait src, toggle UI fully stripped. No dead code or leftover handlers detected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug-fix verification for About-section portrait regeneration is complete and green. Single source of truth = /assets/anil_professional.png (1024x1024, ~572KB). Toggle UI and front/side variant files are gone. If iterating on the portrait again, just replace the file at /app/frontend/public/assets/anil_professional.png; About.jsx hardcodes that path.
rca of the issue: N/A – this iteration is a verification of an already-applied fix, not a bug investigation.
